name: Salad-in-a-Boat
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 2-quart pan, bring water, butter, and salt to a boil.
2. When the butter melts, remove the pan from heat and add flour all at once.
3. Beat until well blended.
4. Return the pan to medium heat and stir rapidly for 1 minute or until a ball forms in the middle of the pan and a film forms on the bottom of the pan.
5. Remove the pan from heat and beat in eggs, one at a time, until the mixture is smooth and glossy.
6. Add cheese and beat until well mixed.
7. Spoon into a greased 9-inch round pan with removable bottom or spring-release sides.
8. Spread evenly over the bottom and up the sides of the pan.
9. Bake crust in a 400 degree oven for 40 minutes or until puffed and brown. Turn off the oven.
10. With a wooden pick, prick the crust in 10 to 12 places; leave the oven closed for about 10 minutes to dry.
11. Remove the pan from the oven and let it cool completely.
12. Remove the crust from the pan.
13. Prepare egg-vegetable salad by stirring all ingredients except eggs together as listed in a bowl.
14. Coarsely chop the 6 hard-cooked eggs; gently fold them into the vegetable mixture.
15. Line the bottom and sides of the boat with spinach leaves.
16. Cut each tomato in half.
17. Pile egg salad over the spinach and garnish with cherry tomatoes.
18. Cut the boat into thick wedges.
